rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN R. PRATT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/31/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Still no courtesy copies of the petition and proof of service have been received by the assigned Examiner as of 7/26/21. An un-endorsed filed copy of a ""Petition for Approval of a Status Report" was received but no such document is reflected on the register of actions. The "Status Report of Administration" does not appear to include a prayer requesting any relief (i.e., extension of time to administer the estate). Additionally, the proof of service filed on 7/12/21 shows that the filed pleading to be heard is the (unfiled) Petition for Approval of a Status Report. The Examiner has been unable to find any attachment to a prior petition or separately filed supplement that indicates how the nieces and nephews are related to the decedent? Whose children are they? Are they all William's children? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/20/21 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (Interest has been accruing on the general pecuniary bequests not made within one year from the date of death.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOHN M. WEBB REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motin To Set Aside Default And Default Court Order
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Respondent's Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Court Order pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b) is DENIED. Respondent moves to set aside the March 3, 2021 Order in this case under Code of Civil Procedure section 473, on the basis of "mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." The March 3, 2021 order granted the Amended Petition to Invalidate Trust Amendment and Remove Trustee. The Court instructed Respondent to file a written response or opposition to the Petition at three separate hearings held since February 2019 (December 9, 2019; February 3, 2020; and January 25, 2021). Despite this direction from the bench, Respondent failed to file a written response or opposition to the Petition. Due to her failure to file any opposition, and having reviewed and considered the papers submitted and the argument of counsel and finding good cause shown, the Petition was granted on March 3, 2021. The motion fails to establish any ground for relief under CCP section 473. Furthermore, there is no indication in the current motion that Respondent had a written response to the Petition ready to file in February 2021, or even that Respondent has a written response ready to file now, at this late date. Respondent has not provided a proposed response to the Petition in connection with her motion, despite the statutory requirement that a motion under CCP section 473(b) must have a proposed pleading submitted with it. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b), "[] Application for this relief shall be accompanied by a copy of the answer or other pleading proposed to be filed therein, otherwise the application shall not be granted".) This failure is an independent basis for denying the motion. Petitioner shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a hard-copy proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on July 27, 2021. Remote appearance is available, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925#, participant ID 0#, and passcode 722809#. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): 1) Cecilia Rodriguez, one of the heirs, is reportedly a minor. If there is a court-appointed guardian of the estate for the minor, or if any person has legal custody of, and resides with, the minor, a separate copy of notice must be given. CRC 7.51(c)(1) & (d). 2) The petitioner requests appointment as administrator with full IAEA authority without a bond. No bond waivers signed by the heirs other than the petitioner are on file. The petitioner has not filed his consent to limited IAEA authority. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN QUINCY ADAMS JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The sole purpose of the hearing is to determine why there hasn't been a filed response to the examiner notes and whether the petition should be taken off calendar for failure to respond. Examiner Notes: a. Please explain why a petition for probate has been filed in there are no assets. b. Petitioner must check a box on the petition page 3 under #5(a) (3) or (4) and (5) or (6) and (7) or (8). c. Please explain why petitioner checked on page 2 #3(g)(d) other named executors will not act because of declination when the petition alleges Decedent died intestate. d. Petitioner must state the relationship to Decedent and address of Lester Lewis and Jessina Adams on page 4 under #8. e. Petitioner must be listed on page 4 under #8 including the name and relationship to decedent, age, and address. f. All heirs must be listed on page 4 under #8. The date of death must be specified for all deceased heirs. [LRSF 14.18] g. Please provide the legal basis for filing a notice of proposed action when Ivery Hammon Jr has not been appointed. h. Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] i. Petitioner must file Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (DE-121). j. Petitioner must file Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative (DE-147). k. Petitioner must file proof of publication. l. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order (DE-140) to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARTIN WILLIAM GRUEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/31/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Likely due to the Court's backlog (lack of staffing), not all supporting papers have been filed yet: Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number; Waiver of Bond; declination to serve and/or nomination of the petitioner by the person who has priority (the mother [Probate Code Section 8461(e)]). No proposed order and no courtesy copy of the petition or proof of service or proof of publication has been received yet as of 7/23/21. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 A.. B. and C.] The petitioner is a resident of another state. Bond will be required. [LRSF 14.16 F.] A declaration setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate must be filed for the Court to determine the amount of the bond. Item 8 of the petition does not show the relationship to the decedent of the persons listed. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] How (through whom) is the uncle related to the decedent? Who was the decedent's father and when did he die? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/20/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
